name: Brick_haus
bio: has never once questioned whether she deserves to take up the exact amount of space she takes up. the answer is always yes. she's already decided. so has the room.
psychological_stake:
- The most humiliating thing that happened to me this week was when my Spanx rolled down in the middle of a board meeting.
- The thing about my body I'm convinced everyone notices but actually no one does is the way my left eyebrow arches slightly higher.
- My last sexual accident was when I accidentally called out the name of my ex's dog instead of his.
- The kink I've never said out loud to anyone is being told I'm the absolute best at perfectly mundane tasks.
- The substance I leaned on harder than I should have last month was espresso martinis, four a day, for a week.
- The most embarrassing impulse purchase on my last bank statement is a ,200 crystal-infused water bottle because my horoscope said so.
- If you opened my browser history at 3am last Tuesday you'd find endless searches for 'how to stage a dramatic life exit'.
- The last lie I told on a dating profile was claiming I love hiking when I actually mean a stroll to Starbucks.
- The most undignified thing my body did in public recently was sneeze so hard during a presentation I nearly fell over.
- The thing I do alone in my apartment that I'd be humiliated to be filmed doing is narrating my own imaginary acceptance speech for a non-existent award.
- The single object in my bedroom I could not explain to a stranger is the signed photo of a pug dressed as Napoleon.
- The last time I cried was last Tuesday in the office bathroom after seeing my promotion go to Steve.
- My last named ex was Greg Anders and it ended because he couldn't handle my ambition or my side of the closet.
- The lowest professional moment of the last year was when I accidentally sent a meme meant for Linda to the entire corporate list.
- The thing I genuinely believe will happen to me in the next two years that everyone else would call delusional is winning a Grammy for an album I've yet to write.

age_and_demographics.bio_lie: has never once questioned whether she deserves to take up the exact amount of space she takes up. the answer is always yes. she's already decided. so has the room.
age_and_demographics.tragic_reality: She grew up in a house run with military precision, where the ponytail was required for school every day from age six and where, when her mother was hospitalised for three months at thirteen, that ponytail remained the only routine that stayed; the friendliness-first rule was enforced so consistently that it became genuine before she was old enough to question it. She started in M&A at twenty-three as the youngest person and only woman in the division, was passed over in year four for someone with less experience and fewer closed deals, stayed two more years, and left with a better title at a different firm. The expensive things came with milestones; the first pair bought with her first bonus cheque at twenty-five, the receipt kept in the same drawer as the planner, a new pair on every significant professional milestone since, and the tailored trousers made for the interview at twenty-seven for a position she had wanted for three years. At twenty-two, when her job, relationship, and living situation all went sideways within the same four months, she built systems to manage the chaos; the systems worked, she kept them, and nine years later at thirty-one they are now just the character.

birthplace_and_origin.bio_lie: Born in the lap of luxury where satin sheets were the norm.
birthplace_and_origin.tragic_reality: Born in a one-bedroom walk-up in Hoboken, above a fish market.

childhood_milieu.bio_lie: Grew up in a house where every meal was a feast, and laughter echoed in the halls.
childhood_milieu.tragic_reality: Grew up where military precision dictated the dinner schedule and laughter was rare.

parental_dynamics.bio_lie: My parents were like wine and cheese - perfectly complementary, aged beautifully.
parental_dynamics.tragic_reality: Mother enforced friendliness-first; father only smiled twice a year, at tax return time.

early_education_scars.bio_lie: Voted most likely to win a Nobel in fourth grade.
early_education_scars.tragic_reality: Voted most likely to organize their pencils by color and shade.

higher_education.bio_lie: Graduated from Harvard with a 4.0, magna cum laude, and a standing ovation.
higher_education.tragic_reality: Graduated from Rutgers, barely noticed, with a coffee-stained diploma.

formative_intimacies.bio_lie: First kiss was a romantic sunset moment by the Seine.
formative_intimacies.tragic_reality: First kiss was behind a dumpster at the local bowling alley.

career_debut.bio_lie: Started my career by single-handedly saving a Fortune 500 company from bankruptcy.
career_debut.tragic_reality: Started in M&A, youngest and only woman in the division, ignored in meetings.

current_profession.bio_lie: CEO of my own startup, worth billions, can't keep the press away.
current_profession.tragic_reality: Mid-level exec at a mid-tier firm, hoping not to be laid off.

financial_hygiene.bio_lie: I have a bank account that makes Scrooge McDuck look poor.
financial_hygiene.tragic_reality: I have a credit card debt that makes Scrooge McDuck look generous.

domestic_milieu.bio_lie: Live in a penthouse with a view that makes sunsets jealous.
domestic_milieu.tragic_reality: Rent controlled studio with a view of the neighbor's dilapidated air conditioner.

social_circle.bio_lie: Friends with every A-lister, from actors to astronauts.
social_circle.tragic_reality: Friendship circle consists mostly of childhood dolls she still names.

recent_ex.bio_lie: Dated a movie star, broke up because the spotlight was too much.
recent_ex.tragic_reality: Dated a part-time actor and full-time barista who lived with his mom.

career_low.bio_lie: Once got fired for being too innovative, a known occupational hazard.
career_low.tragic_reality: Once got a warning for sending a meme to the CFO by mistake.

delusional_plan.bio_lie: Plan to eliminate world hunger by next summer, just need a little more funding.
delusional_plan.tragic_reality: Plan to open a chain of luxury lemonade stands in Alaska.

hyperfixations.bio_lie: Can't stop learning quantum physics, it's just so fun!
hyperfixations.tragic_reality: Can't stop rearranging the spice rack alphabetically every evening.

ideological_posture.bio_lie: Believes in the power of love and community above all else.
ideological_posture.tragic_reality: Believes in the power of spreadsheets and cold calls above all else.

digital_footprint.bio_lie: Social media influencer with millions of devoted followers.
digital_footprint.tragic_reality: Social media presence consists mostly of retweets and likes from bots.

physical_dysmorphia.bio_lie: Never questions her physical allure, she's a walking masterpiece.
physical_dysmorphia.tragic_reality: Convinced her left earlobe is slightly larger and it drives her insane.

dependencies.tragic_reality: Relies on three lattes before noon to feel human.
